HGSS.649.0001:
Huh? I can’t hear you very well!
I think we’ve got a bad connection.\f
Call me back from somewhere else!
HGSS.649.0002:
Hello? How are you?
I’m doing great as usual!\f
HGSS.649.0003:
There are so many stronger Gym
Leaders than me out there.\f
You’re trying to visit every Gym around
the region, aren’t you?\r
That’s great! Keep up the good work!
HGSS.649.0004:
On Thursdays around noon, I’m
usually free.\r
If you want to see my Bug-type Pokémon\r
again, you should call me then!
HGSS.649.0005:
Oh, you called at just the right time!
I’m not doing anything right now.\r
Wanna battle again? [0200,0000]
HGSS.649.0006:
All right!\f
See you at the Fighting Dojo in
Saffron City!
HGSS.649.0007:
I see...
Well, there’s no helping that...
HGSS.649.0008:
Huh?
It’s great that you want to battle,\r
but don’t you have other plans?\f
You should take care of those plans
first, and then give me a call.
HGSS.649.0009:
All right, I’ll be waiting for you at the
Fighting Dojo in Saffron City!\r
Race you there!
HGSS.649.0010:
Yeah, this is Bugsy...
What’s up?\f
HGSS.649.0011:
Hey, didn’t we make plans to meet at
the Fighting Dojo in Saffron City for a\r
battle? Did you forget or something?
HGSS.649.0012:
You know, everyone’s really into
Bug-type Pokémon when they’re kids.\f
But then for some reason, as people
grow up, at some point they all start\r
to like Bug-type Pokémon less and less.\f
How about you?
Do you still like Bug-type Pokémon?\f
Even if I grow up I’ll never stop liking
Bug-type Pokémon, no way!
HGSS.649.0013:
My Gym has such a different trick to it,
doesn’t it?\f
It may seem like it was really difficult
to get a trick like that set up...\r
But the truth is, it was actually\r
pretty easy.\f
First we ran the ropes through the
Gym, then all the Pokémon helped us\r
finish everything else up!
HGSS.649.0014:
I think I’ve already mentioned this to
you, but did you know I’m doing\r
research on Bug-type Pokémon?\f
There’s so many different kinds, it’s a
bit difficult, you know?\f
I’m just studying Bug-type Pokémon,
and yet it’s still this difficult.\f
People like Professor Elm and
Professor Oak who study all the\r
different types of Pokémon are really\r
something else, you know?
